data.title: Stifel Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.07, revenue of $1.48B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Stifel Financial press release (SF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.48B (+17.5% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • The Company reported net revenues of $1.48 billion, the second best in its history, driven by higher investment banking revenues, asset management revenues, transactional revenues, net interest income, and the recognition of a gain on the sale of Stifel Independent Advisors, LLC, which closed on February 2, 2026.
  • Record asset management revenues, up 12% over the year-ago quarter.
  • Client assets of $538.7 billion, up 11% over the year-ago quarter.
  • Over the last twelve months, recruited trailing twelve-month production totaled approximately $80 million.

data.title: Moody's beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Moody's press release (MCO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.33 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $2.1B (+9.4% Y/Y) beats by $40M,and 6% on an organic constant currency2 basis.
  • MCO’s operating margin was 44.3%; MCO’s Adjusted Operating Margin2 expanded by 150 bps to 53.2%.
  • Operating cash flow of $939 million up 24%; Free Cash Flow2 of $844 million up 26%.
  • Accelerated capital returns: $1.7 billion share repurchases and dividends in the first quarter; raised full-year share repurchase guidance to approximately $2.5 billion1.
  • Reaffirmed guidance for full-year 2026 MCO revenue growth1 in the high-single digit percent range and Adjusted Diluted EPS1 within the range of $16.40 to $17.00 vs. $16.68 consensus.
  • Named Christina Kosmowski as CEO of Moody’s Analytics, effective June 2026.

data.title: Boston Scientific cuts full-year outlook despite Q1 beat
data.description:
Shares of Boston Scientific (BSX) traded lower early in the premarket on Wednesday after the MedTech firm lowered its full-year outlook despite reporting better-than-expected financials for Q1 2026.

The company projects its 2026 revenue to grow at roughly 7.0% YoY - 8.5% YoY on a reported basis compared to 10.5% - 11.5% estimated with its Q4 2025 financials in February and ~11% YoY growth implied in the consensus.

Its non-GAAP earnings per share for the year are expected to reach $3.34 - $3.41 compared to $3.43 - $3.49 previously and $3.45 in the consensus.

As for the current quarter, the Marlborough, Massachusetts-based firm expects approximately 5.5% YoY–7.5% YoY growth and $0.82–$0.84 in adjusted EPS compared to ~10% YoY growth and $0.86 in the consensus, respectively.

“We remain focused on executing our long-term strategy and advancing our differentiated pipeline to drive meaningful impact for patients, physicians, and hospital systems,” CEO Mike Mahoney remarked.

However, for Q1, the company did beat Street forecasts, reporting $5.2B in revenue with ~12% YoY growth and $0.80 of adjusted EPS, exceeding the consensus by $30M and $0.01, respectively.

Boston Scientific’s (BSX) Cardiovascular unit led the outperformance, adding $3.5B to the topline with ~14% YoY growth, while Endoscopy net sales reached $736M, implying ~9% YoY growth, exceeding $722.1M in the consensus, according to Bloomberg data.

However, its Urology unit trailed expectations, adding $646M in net sales with a ~2% YoY growth, missing the consensus of $682.9M.

data.title: Sam's Club introduces a one-hour delivery option
data.description:
Sam's Club introduced a one-hour delivery option, according to an update on its website. The membership warehouse chain noted that the delivery offer costs just $10 extra for Plus members or $17 for Club members. Notably, there is no order minimum. The one-hour delivery service is subject to club hours, inventory availability, and geographic coverage.

Sam’s Club had a strong 2025 based on reporting from Walmart (WMT). Sam’s Club U.S. generated $90.2B in net sales in FY25, up from $86.2B in fiscal FY24, and membership income remained a meaningful profit driver for the segment. E-commerce at Sam's Club was up 24% year over year in FY25. 

Looking ahead, at Walmart's (WMT) 2025 Investment Community Meeting, Sam's Club said it aimed to double membership and more than double sales and profit over the next 8 to 10 years, signaling confidence from management and the board in the long-term momentum for the business.

Shares of Walmart (WMT) are up more than 16% on a year-to-date basis as a defensive positioning by investors has provided a boost. Walmart (WMT) is not expected to report first-quarter earnings until the third week of May.

data.title: Avis Budget continues to soar, Michael Burry calls it ‘dumb luck’
data.description:
Avis Budget Group (CAR) shares jumped as much as 9% in premarket trading Wednesday, putting the stock on track for a fifth straight day of gains.

CAR’s stock is up nearly 390% in April, on course to mark its best monthly performance on record. The car rental company has rallied over 565% in the past month alone.

“Avis Budget (CAR) surged 500% in five weeks, driven by an unprecedented short squeeze rooted in extreme float mechanics,” SA columnist Doug Collins said.

Avis Budget Group (CAR) currently has 35.32M shares outstanding and a short interest of 25.55%.

“Two hedge funds, SRS and Pentwater, accumulated over 100% economic interest via shares and swaps, locking up 82% of CAR's float. Short interest exceeded available float, triggering a gamma squeeze as dealers and shorts scrambled to cover, further fueling the rally,” Collins explained.

Responding to the rally, Wall Street investor Michael Burry called the situation “dumb luck” in his Substack.

“That is something we call dumb luck. Through the lens of history from Joe Kennedy’s shoe shiner to this case, speculative markets had never been short of that,” he wrote.

data.title: Teledyne beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Teledyne press release (TDY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $5.80 beats by $0.33.
  • Revenue of $1.56B (+7.6% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • First quarter cash from operations of $234.0 million and free cash flow of $204.3 million.
  • Raising full year 2026 GAAP diluted earnings per share outlook to $20.08 to $20.44 compared with the prior outlook of $19.76 to $20.22, and raising full year 2026 non-GAAP earnings per share outlook to $23.85 to $24.15, compared with the prior outlook of $23.45 to $23.85 vs. consensus of $20.17.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: AT&T advanced connectivity revenue up on service and equipment growth; postpaid churn rises to 0.89%
Jacobs joint venture secures contract to design five stations for Sydney Metro West
data.description:
• Jacobs (J), GHD and WSP have been appointed in a joint venture to design five new underground stations for Sydney Metro West project.
  • The joint venture has been engaged to provide integrated engineering design services for Stations Package West – the stations at Westmead, North Strathfield, Burwood North, Five Dock and The Bays.

data.title: BankUnited GAAP EPS of $0.83 misses by $0.13, revenue of $273.69M misses by $11.27M
data.description:
• BankUnited press release (BKU): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.83 misses by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $273.69M (+7.2% Y/Y) misses by $11.27M.
  • Total Deposits excluding brokered: up $1.4 billion from a year ago, and up $277 million from prior quarter.
  • The provision for credit losses totaled $24.6 million for the quarter, compared to $25.6 million for the prior quarter and $15.1 million for Q1 2025.

data.title: Costco's Kirkland energy drink is a disruptor despite limited supply
data.description:
Costco's (COST) new Kirkland Signature Sparkling Energy Drink is creating a stir in the industry, despite its limited availability. The zero‑sugar, lightly carbonated 12 oz energy drink could be positioned as a value alternative to brands like Celsius (CELH) if it is rolled out broadly. It comes in a 24‑can variety pack featuring peach, orange, and tropical flavors, with each can containing about 200 mg of caffeine, roughly 10 calories, added vitamins, and no sugar.Pricing has been one of the biggest talking points since each case is typically around $16.99, which substantially undercuts name‑brand competitors whose multipacks at Costco (COST) often land at roughly double that price on a per‑can basis.Notably, early reception has been broadly positive among Costco (COST) shoppers and energy drink fans, especially on taste and value. Online Costco (COST) communities and energy drink forums have been recommending it heavily to existing Celsius and Alani drinkers who want a cheaper daily option. The launch drew enough attention that it briefly rattled investors in Celsius Holdings (CELH).Evercore ISI reported that at a big recent regional meeting for Costco (COST) managers in the Northeast, all the stores were "clamoring to get their hands on the product" as soon as possible, which could signal a larger rollout is likely.

Elsewhere in the energy drink category, an increase in Red Bull promotional intensitywas later confirmed by Evercore through checks at Target (TGT), Amazon (AMZN), and Stop & Shop locations.

The recent developments in the energy drink sector could put pricing pressure on Celsius Holdings (CELH) and Monster Beverage (MNST).
